title: Cleistes triflora
intro: Cleistes triflora, also known as the Brazilian orchid or three-flower cleistes, is a type of orchid that belongs to the subfamily Spiranthoideae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Cleistes triflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cleistes triflora include infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cleistes triflora is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Cleistes and the family Orchidaceae. Cleistes triflora is commonly known as the three-flowered Cleistes or Three-flowered Orchid. The classification of Cleistes triflora is within the subfamily Vanilloideae. This orchid is native to Brazil.
